name: Sendero de las Grandes Encinas (PRC-BU 121) — circular desde Solarana
region_intro: Hiking around Solarana offers access to diverse landscapes, characterized by natural gorges and areas surrounded by nature. The region features varied terrain, including woodlands and paths that lead to panoramic viewpoints. While not featuring high peaks, the trails provide opportunities to explore geological formations and scenic routes. Best hiking trails around Solarana The most popular hiking route is Mataviejas Gorge Trail, an easy 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This path explores the natural gorge with minimal elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mataviejas Gorge Loop Trail, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.4 km) path. This route offers a longer exploration of the gorge area, featuring varied terrain and more significant elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Sendero de las Grandes Encinas (PRC-BU 121) — circular desde Solarana, a 5.9 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through oak woodlands, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Solarana is defined by natural gorges, oak woodlands, and paths leading to panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate hikes. The routes in Solarana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Nearly 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Solarana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Solarana?
A: Solarana offers a selection of over 10 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging moderate routes.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Solarana?
A: The hiking trails around Solarana are characterized by diverse landscapes, including dramatic natural gorges like the Mataviejas Gorge, serene oak woodlands, and paths that lead to panoramic viewpoints. The region provides opportunities to explore unique geological formations and scenic routes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Solarana has several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281815249/mataviejas-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5-mile (5.6 km) path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed exploration of the natural gorge.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Solarana?
A: Absolutely. Many hikers enjoy circular routes for their convenience. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281903963/sendero-de-las-grandes-encinas-prc-bu-121-circular-desde-solarana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de las Grandes Encinas (PRC-BU 121) — circular desde Solarana&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 5.9-mile (9.4 km) trail through oak woodlands.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I discover along the hiking trails?
A: While hiking around Solarana, you can explore significant natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, known for its dramatic landscapes. You might also find paths leading to scenic viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5517941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Arcos&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views of the surrounding area.

Q: Are there longer or more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer and more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282042421/mataviejas-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.4-mile (10.4 km) path that offers a more extensive exploration of the gorge area with significant elevation changes. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292353728/sendero-del-cura-merino-prc-bu-124-fontioso-circular-desde-villoviado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Cura Merino (PRC-BU-124)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Solarana?
A: The hiking routes in Solarana are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the natural gorges, and the peaceful oak woodlands.

Q: Is Solarana a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Solarana offers trails suitable for families. The easier routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281815249/mataviejas-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are generally well-maintained and provide an accessible way for families to enjoy the natural surroundings without excessive difficulty.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Solarana?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage at trailheads for any specific restrictions regarding pets, particularly in protected areas.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views in the area?
A: Yes, the region around Solarana features paths that lead to panoramic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5517941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Arcos&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint where you can enjoy expansive vistas of the landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Solarana?
A: The best time to hike in Solarana generally depends on personal preference, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summers can be warm, while winters are typically cooler, though hiking is possible year-round.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads for hikers?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead is not universally available, many rural hiking areas in Spain typically offer informal or designated parking spots near popular starting points. It's advisable to check route descriptions on komoot for details on specific trails.
tips: Small town of just 100 inhabitants. The point of interest is the Church of the Assumption of Our Lady. It dates from the late 15th century or early 16th century and is in the Gothic style.
Section of the Great Holm Oak Trail that passes through a beautiful holm oak forest. During the route we can see among the oaks remains of old barns that are now in disuse.
